Passed enforced test review-dne-part-3 on RHEL 9.4 / x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-153vm4: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/7546a522-64cf-487e-ba29-9ec4d24d53a7 ran 3 tests.

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Are you planning to push a different patch for a fix?

It isn't clear why it is necessary to revert this patch instead of just fixing the existing code?  Is the reverted patch worse than the problem it was trying to fix?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> Are you planning to push a different patch for a fix?
> 
> It isn't clear why it is necessary to revert this patch instead of just fixing the existing code?  

> Is the reverted patch worse than the problem it was trying to fix?
Revert needs because kiblnd_abort_connreq had a release a connection reference reserved to the event. So any event after it will had access to the freed code. and old code don't needs. In general most of kiblnd_deregister_connreq may removed.

Fix is simple: just call rdma_disconnect to move an CM QP into error state it blocks an event generation because of internal QP state.

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> Are you planning to push a different patch for a fix?
> 
> It isn't clear why it is necessary to revert this patch instead of just fixing the existing code?  Is the reverted patch worse than the problem it was trying to fix?

I prefer to the remove all kiblnd_deregister_connreq() from event handler to find all places where rdma_disconnect lost. Because it hide a problem when connection (rdma_id->context) object reference released by kiblnd_connd, but event queue isn't stoped.

It had one of objections for original patch.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Builds for x86_64 centos7,rhel8.5 successful
Job output URL: https://testing.whamcloud.com/gerrit-janitor/49683/results.html

 Commencing initial testing: runtests-ssk@ldiskfs+SharedKey sanity-lnet@ldiskfs+DNE sanity-lnet@zfs

Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

> I prefer to the remove all kiblnd_deregister_connreq() from event handler to find all places where rdma_disconnect lost. Because it hide a problem when connection (rdma_id->context) object reference released by kiblnd_connd, but event queue isn't stoped.
> 
> It had one of objections for original patch.

I was not calling rdma_disconnect() here because in this case, the connection is not established. But I have no objection to add it in kiblnd_abort_connreq() to set the CM QP state in error state.

Your crash in the LU-18755 ticket matches LU-18260 calltrace. It seems you were testing without the "LU-18260 o2iblnd: fix race between REJ vs kiblnd_connd".

Are you able to reproduce a crash/memory corruption/use-after-free with LU-18260's patch?

Note one more thing, there is bug in the MOFED that corrupt kernel memory in case of ARP update (see LU-18364 or https://lore.kernel.org/all/a3bbcb1d-00bc-4218-85da-291bf368770d@linux.dev/T/).
Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 3:

(2 comments)

You said in your comment that the connection is freed in your crash report. But, I am not sure that we get the right root cause. I am thinking of LU-18364 because this is a corruption of the MOFED event queue if we receive several ARP update (this generate UNREACHABLE event).

Can you check the different internal states to compare those to the expected:
- connection state
- conn->ibc_list
- id_priv->state
- id_priv->handler_mutex
- if kiblnd_connd thread is destroying our connection.
- if the cmid is also freed

commit.subject: LU-18755 o2iblnd: lets disconnect at anycases
commit.message:
LU-18755 o2iblnd: lets disconnect at anycases

rdma_disconnect must called to move CM QP into error state
to avoid event generation. LU-18260 needs reverted because it have
access to the already freed memory.

Fixes: 0b8c18d ("LU-17480 o2iblnd: add a timeout for rdma_connect")
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey.lyashkov@hpe.com>
Change-Id: Ib4aeda6932081422d418380fd811f586e8c17a8a
